headline: Cha-cha via people's initiative bill filed in Senate  GMA News Online
text: A bill enabling charter change (Cha-cha) through people's initiative (PI) and penalizing the use or promise of money to gather signatures has been filed in the Senate.Senate Minority Leader Aquilino "Koko" Pimentel III filed Senate Bill No. 2595 or the proposed People's Initiative and Referendum Act of 2024 to replace Republic Act 6735.RA 6735 was passed in 1994 but was deemed "inadequate" by the Supreme Court to cover the system of initiative in amending the charter and "fails to provide" sufficient standards for subordinate legislation on the part of the Commission on Elections, Pimentel noted."As a measure to not only fill in the gaps found in RA No. 6735 but to also update the system of people's initiative and referendum to reflect the present-day political conditions, it is proper that this bill be introduced as a new measure, rather than an amendment to the existing law," Pimentel said in his explanatory note.Under SB 2595, any registered voter or group of voters may initiate a people's initiative by filing a verified petition with the Commission on Elections (Comelec).The petition to amend the constitution through PI should contain the following:-Complete name, address, and personal circumstances of the proponents, to be called the petitioners-Complete text of the proposed amendment to the Constitution-Reasons-Signature sheets signed by at least 12 percent of the total number of registered voters, of which every legislative district must be represented by at least 3 percent of the registered voters therein-Starting date when the signatures were collectedThe petition should also include the following statement, among others:-The signature sheets attached to the petition contain the complete text of the proposed amendment to the Constitution-The proposed amendment was explained to the signatories in a language or dialect known to them before their signatures were affixed-The reasons for the proposed amendment to each signatory were thoroughly explained before signing it-The petition was filed within 18 months from the date the signature was affixed-That no money or anything of value was given, offered, or promised to be given to procure signatures-That no funds were received from those disqualified to contribute for purposes of partisan political activity under the Omnibus Election Code and other election lawsThe bill mandates the Comelec to determine the sufficiency in form and substance of the petition and verify the number of signatures and their authenticity within 90 days.The decision of the Comelec may be brought to the Supreme Court by a special civil action for certiorari.A plebiscite will be scheduled by the Comelec not earlier than 60 days but not later than 90 days from its certification of the sufficiency of the petition.Prior to the date of the plebiscite, the Comelec shall publish the complete text of the proposed amendment to the Constitution once a week for two consecutive weeks in a newspaper of general nationwide circulation.The amendment to the constitution shall take effect immediately upon its ratification by a majority of the votes cast in a plebiscite.SB 2595 likewise prohibits a petition proposing a revision of the Constitution. The bill defines revision as a change or alteration of the Constitution which changes the basic constitutional structure or which affects the functioning of various parts of agencies within the constitutional structure.Any person who gives, offers, or promises money or anything of value, any office or employment, franchise or grant, whether in public or private, to induce anyone to sign the petition may face six to nine years of jail time.If it is committed by a public officer, a jail time of nine to 12 years shall be meted. All his benefits will be forfeited and he will be perpetually disqualified from holding public office.Six to nine years imprisonment will likewise be slapped on any person who directly or indirectly uses public funds or money held in trust by public financing institutions or by government offices, and government publication arms for any signature campaign for PI.Any public officer who directly or indirectly solicits signatures in support of a petition to push for PI will also face nine to 12 years in jail.—LDF, GMA Integrated News

headline: PCSO list showed one bettor won 20 times in a month —Tulfo  GMA News Online
text:
Senator Raffy Tulfo on Tuesday disclosed that a bettor's name appeared to have won the lotto 20 times in one month, citing a list submitted by the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO).
"'Yung PCSO nagbigay ho sa akin ng listahan...Ito nga po ang sabi ko medyo nakakataas ng kilay, meron doon na isang tao nanalo ng 20 times in one month. Meron dun 10 times in one month," Tulfo said in an interview on Dobol B TV.
(The PCSO has given me a list of winners and what caught our attention is this one bettor who won 20 times in just one month. There's another who won 10 times in one month.)  "Siguro baka magkapangalan pero still pare-pareho 'yung premyo ang pinanalunan...Paulit-ulit in one month... Lalong tumambak ang mga questions," he added.
(Maybe there are some winners who just have the same name but the games that they won were also the same.)
Tulfo is leading the investigation into the integrity of PCSO games. Apart from the list of winners, the PCSO also submitted to the Senate panel the documents on the lotto games, including the tax records, of the jackpot winners."Bakit tuwing draw, may nananalo na? Ngayon, walang mintis. Samantalang nu'ng panahon na wala pa tong e-lotto, ang tagal bago panalunan. Inaabot ng isang buwan, dalawang buwan," Tulfo said.  
(Why is there always a winner in every draw? Before there was e-lotto it took a month or two before someone hit the jackpot.)
'Easy to investigate'
In a separate interview on Dobol B TV, PCSO General Manager Mel Robles said they will look into the observation that was disclosed by Tulfo and they can also investigate the matter. "I wouldn't know [that]. Ako, personally, hindi ko po tintingnan 'yon. Pero I'll check, I'll look into that kung meron pong ganon, kasi hindi naman po kami tumitingin na n'yan after namin maibigay e," Robles said.  
(I wouldn't know that information because I don't look into it frequently. But I'll verify this information.)"Puwede imbestigahan 'yan. 'Di naman mahirap imbestigahan 'yan e," he went on.
(We can investigate that. It's not that hard to investigate that matter.)Robles had repeatedly denied that the PCSO can manipulate the results of lotto games amid allegations and senators' questions about the frequent winnings of lotto games.“We would like to assure you that you can never, never manipulate it. Kaya nga allowed kami mag-bet, Mr. Chair. Even I can bet because it’s beyond me. Even if we wanted to, with the system we have, we cannot,” Robles said in a previous Senate hearing.“I take exception na kayang panalunin. Definitely, madami pong tataya kung malaki ang jackpot. ‘Yun po naman ang objective namin– to really bring in bettors,” the PCSO official added.
(I take exception to the allegation. Definitely, more people will place bets because of the higher jackpot price. That was really our objective, to bring in more bettors.)Robles also previously explained that there was a surplus fund to augment the lotto prizes and they did it as a “marketing campaign” to encourage the public to place their bets for higher prizes. —VAL, GMA Integrated News

headline: Ruru Madrid, Bianca Umali hope to bring joy to global Pinoys in Canada  GMA News Online
text:
Ruru Madrid and Bianca Umali are set to entertain global Pinoys in Canada in April, along with the BarDa love team of Barbie Forteza and David Licauco, the JulieVer love team of Julie Anne San Jose and Rayver Cruz, and comedian and TV host Boobay.
In a Sparkle media conference on Tuesday, Ruru said that it's an honor to perform and give joy to fellow Pinoys abroad who always welcome them with open arms.
"Iba 'yung sabik na napapakita nila. Iba 'yung init ng pagtanggap every time na nagpe-perform ka sa harapan nila," the "Black Rider" star said.
"So, para mapasama at maging isang instrumento na makapagpasaya ng ating masisipag na OFW ay malaking karangalan po iyon para sa amin. Ayun po talaga ang nilu-look forward namin," he added.
Aside from bringing joy to the fans, Bianca is also looking forward to bringing "home" to them, despite being away in their home country. 
"Sabik talaga silang maka-uwi at siyempre focus sila sa kung ano mang trabaho nila do'n, so they miss home and I think the main goal here is makapagpasaya doon and bring home to them," she said. 
"Kami ang pupunta, kami ang yayakap sa kanila, for them to feel na home is tayong mga Pinoy na magkakasama. Hindi porket magkakalayo tayo ay hindi posibleng magkasiyahan tayo at maramdaman natin ang pagmamahal at init ng bansa natin kapag magkakasama tayo," she added. 
The show will be held on April 5 in Calgary and April 7 in Toronto. It will be directed by Johnny Manahan.
Ruru and Bianca confirmed their relationship in 2022. The couple starred in their first project together, "The Write One," last year.
Julie Anne and Rayver, meanwhile, have been making fans kilig with their jamming sessions and dance covers online. They exchanged "I love yous" during their JulieVerse concert in 2022 and also shared the stage in Limitless Live.
Barbie and David rose to popularity as a love team when they played Fidel and Klay in historical portal fantasy series "Maria Clara at Ibarra." They also starred in the special limited series "Maging Sino Ka Man."
Boobay is a co-host of "The Boobay and Tekla Show," which airs on Sundays at 10:05 p.m. after "Kapuso Mo, Jessica Soho."

headline: Easterlies to affect most parts of PH  GMA News Online
text:
Cloudy skies with isolated rain showers will prevail in most parts of the country due to the easterlies, state weather bureau PAGASA said Tuesday afternoon.
According to PAGASA's 24-hour weather forecast, the easterlies will bring cloudy skies with scattered rain showers and thunderstorms over Caraga and Davao Region.
The Northeast Monsoon will cause cloudy skies with light rains over Cagayan Valley, Cordillera Administrative Region, and Aurora, and partly cloudy to cloudy skies with isolated light rains over Ilocos Region and the rest of Central Luzon.
The rest of the country, meanwhile, will experience partly cloudy to cloudy skies with isolated rain showers or thunderstorms because of the combined effects of the easterlies and localized thunderstorms.
Luzon and the eastern sections of Visayas and Mindanao will experience moderate to strong winds with moderate to rough coastal waters, while the rest of the country is forecast to have light to moderate winds with slight to moderate coastal waters.
Sunrise in Metro Manila will be at 6:06 a.m. on Wednesday.  —Jiselle Anne Casucian/VBL, GMA Integrated News

headline: PPP Center, DOTr start sounding for EDSA busway privatization  GMA News Online
text:
The Public-Private Partnership (PPP) Center and the Department of Transportation (DOTr) commenced an initial market sounding on the plan to privatize the operations of the EDSA Busway service to gauge stakeholders’ interest in participating in the project.
In a news release on Tuesday, the PPP Center said that, together with the DOTr, it commenced the preliminary market-sounding activity for the EDSA Busway project on March 8, 2024.
Approximately 60 private sector participants and five government agencies attended the market-sounding activity.
“This activity seeks to provide an overview of the proposed PPP project, gather initial feedback and valuable insights from private sector participants, and identify potential issues or concerns affecting project viability,” the PPP Center said.
The agency said the EDSA Busway project aimed to enhance accessibility, safety, and reliability, and to shorten travel time for commuters along the EDSA corridor and other major and feeder roads within Metro Manila.
“The potential scope of responsibility of the private sector partner could encompass financing, design, construction, operations, and maintenance of upgraded bus stations and facilities, procurement of bus fleet, operations and maintenance of busway system,” the PPP Center said.
As early as October 2022, the DOTr called for a feasibility study for the proposed privatization of the EDSA Busway System.
Transportation Secretary Jaime Bautista had said he wanted the planned privatization of the EDSA Busway service accomplished through solicited bidding.
Meanwhile, the PPP Center signed a technical assistance agreement with the DOTr, including project preparation and transaction advisory services, on April 5, 2023.
The agency looked to complete the feasibility study of the project by midyear. — DVM, GMA Integrated News

headline: David Licauco on meeting Kathryn Bernardo 'I'm super happy to see her as a fan'  GMA News Online
text:
David Licauco is a Kathryn Bernardo fanboy! 
In an interview with the members of the press on Tuesday, the Pambansang Ginoo said that he watched Kathryn's movies with her former boyfriend and on-screen partner Daniel Padilla. 
David and Kathryn met during the Bench Fashion Week this weekend and posed for photos together.
"I'm super happy to see her as a fan," the Sparkle actor said.
When asked if he is open to working with the actress, David enthusiastically answered, "Yes." 
"Oo naman. Siyempre. Anything naman na work, I'll do it," he said.
David and Kathryn, who are both endorsers of Bench were among the celebrities who graced the fashion event over the weekend.
Both of them flew to Australia to watch Taylor Swift's "The Eras Tour." David watched in Sydney, while Kathryn watched in Melbourne.
David is set to star in GMA Network's upcoming historical-action drama "Pulang Araw," alongside Barbie Forteza, Alden Richards, and Sanya Lopez.
He recently starred with Barbie in "Maging Sino Ka Man," which came after the success of their love team "Maria Clara at Ibarra."
David and Barbie will also be part of Sparkle's Canada Tour this April along with Ruru Madrid and Bianca Umali, Julie Anne San Jose and Rayver Cruz, and Boobay.
Meanwhile, Kathryn and Daniel made headlines last November when they announced their breakup, ending their 11-year relationship.

headline: PHIVOLCS issues vog alert on Taal Volcano  GMA News Online
text:
A vog alert has been issued on Taal Volcano on Tuesday, according to the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (PHIVOLCS).Volcanic smog was reported over the Taal Caldera at 2 p.m., while a total of 6,837 tonnes per day of volcanic sulfur dioxide was recorded since Monday.Data from the Batangas Provincial Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office indicated the following areas have been affected by vog:
PHIVOLCS advised the public to take the necessary steps to protect themselves against vog, such as limiting outdoor activities and wearing N95 face masks.
Earlier on February 19, Taal Volcano emitted over 14,000 tonnes of volcanic sulfur dioxide.Despite this, PHIVOLCS director Teresito Bacolcol said that an eruption is unlikely due to other volcanic indicators not being present.Taal Volcano remains at Alert Level 1 or Low-level unrest as of Tuesday, 12 a.m.—RF, GMA Integrated News

headline: Senate approves Negros Island Region bill on final reading  GMA News Online
text:
The Senate on Tuesday approved on third and final reading a bill seeking to create the Negros Island Region.
Senate Bill 2507 got 22 affirmative votes, zero negative votes, and zero abstentions.
The proposed Negros Island Region will be composed of Negros Occidental, Bacolod City, Negros Oriental, and Siquijor.
Senator JV Ejercito, sponsor of the measure, said the goal is for the six clusters of regional offices to be divided between the two halves of the island to ensure the effective delivery of services.
He added that the proposed legislation will strengthen local autonomy and accelerate the economic, cultural, and social and development of the region.
The counterpart measure of the bill in the House of Representatives was approved on final reading in March 2023.
In 2017, then-president Rodrigo Duterte issued Executive Order 38, formally dissolving the two-year-old region created under the Aquino administration. — BM, GMA Integrated News

headline: Cat that fell into vat of chemicals sparks alert in Japan  GMA News Online
text:
TOKYO — Locals in a city in western Japan have been told not to approach or touch a missing cat that fell into a vat of dangerous chemicals and then scampered off into the night.
An employee at a metal plating plant in Fukuyama arrived for work on Monday morning to find feline paw prints leading away from the container, the firm said.
Grainy security camera footage released by the company shows the furry fugitive fleeing from the factory, apparently unharmed.
The vat, three meters (10 feet) deep, contained hexavalent chromium, which is carcinogenic and can cause skin inflammation.
"We immediately alerted police, the Fukuyama city and neighbors near our factory," a representative from the company told AFP, without wishing to be named.
"The incident woke us up to the need to take measures to prevent small animals like cats from sneaking in, which is something we had never anticipated before," he said.
City authorities told locals to avoid touching the animal and to inform police immediately if they come across it.
No sightings had been reported as of Tuesday afternoon. — Agence France-Presse
